title: dryopteris decurrenti-alata
intro: Dryopteris decurrenti-alata, also known as the Decurrent Wood Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. It is a perennial plant native to Asia, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Dryopteris decurrenti-alata can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are said to have health benefits such as improving digestion, relieving menstrual cramps, and boosting the immune system.
